text: this is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English. I'm Neil.
zh: [0] 这里是BBC英语教学的《六分钟英语》节目,我是Neil。

text: And I'm Georgie. Some kids hate eating broccoli.
zh: [1] 我是Georgie。有些孩子讨厌吃西兰花。

text: For others it's mushrooms and worst of all, of course, are Brussels sprouts.
zh: [2] 有些孩子讨厌蘑菇,而最糟糕的当然是抱子甘蓝。

text: Every parent of young children knows what food they will and won't eat.
zh: [3] 每个有小孩的家长都清楚孩子爱吃什么、不爱吃什么。

text: What food did you dislike as a child, Georgie?
zh: [4] Georgie,你小时候不喜欢吃什么食物?

text: Ooh, when I was little I used to absolutely hate nuts and avocados,
zh: [5] 哦,我小时候特别讨厌坚果和牛油果,

text: but now, as an adult, I absolutely love both of those things.
zh: [6] 但现在长大了,我特别喜欢这两样东西。

text: I'm not surprised, they're delicious! What about you, Neil?
zh: [7] 我一点也不惊讶,它们确实很好吃!Neil,你呢?

text: Did you dislike any foods? Do you know what? I think I liked most things.
zh: [8] 你小时候讨厌什么食物吗?说实话,我觉得我什么都爱吃。

text: Family dinner time can be fun – a chance to reconnect, hear about everyone's day and share a meal.
zh: [9] 家庭晚餐时光可以很愉快——这是重新联络感情、了解彼此一天生活、共享美食的机会。

text: But when When children refuse to eat the food that's been cooked, the result is often conflict rather than family fun.
zh: [10] 但当孩子拒绝吃做好的饭菜时,结果往往是引发冲突,而不是其乐融融。

text: BBC Radio 4 programme The Food Chain asked child nutritionist and mother of two, Charlotte Stirling -Reed, whether her kids eat everything she gives them.
zh: [11] BBC第四台节目《The Food Chain》采访了儿童营养师、两个孩子的母亲Charlotte Stirling-Reed,询问她的孩子是否什么都吃。

text: They definitely don't. We go through multiple periods of up and downs where their appetite is much lower and then all of a sudden it comes right back.
zh: [12] 他们当然不是什么都吃。孩子的食欲会经历多次起伏,有时胃口很小,然后突然又恢复正常。

text: It depends on the weather, what they've been doing that day. It can depend on their mood.
zh: [13] 这取决于天气,取决于他们那天做了什么,也取决于他们的心情。

text: At dinner time, Charlotte's children go through ups and downs, a phrase meaning that a mixture of good and bad things happen to them.
zh: [14] 在晚餐时间,Charlotte的孩子们会经历“ups and downs”(起起落落),这个短语指好事和坏事交替发生。

text: Sometimes they eat everything happily, other times they don't.
zh: [15] 有时他们会开心地吃光所有食物,有时却不会。

text: It depends on many things including their day, the weather and their mood.
zh: [16] 这取决于很多因素,包括他们那天的经历、天气和心情。

text: A person's mood is the way they're feeling at a particular time.
zh: [17] 一个人的“mood”(心情)指的是他们当时的感受状态。

text: So, how can parents make dinner time something everyone enjoys?
zh: [18] 那么,家长该如何让晚餐时间变成大家都享受的时光呢?

text: In this episode, we'll be hearing from three experts on young children's eating habits and learning some useful new words and phrases too.
zh: [19] 在这一期节目中,我们将听三位专家谈论幼儿的饮食习惯,并学习一些实用的新单词和短语。

text: And remember, you'll find all the vocabulary from this episode, along with a quiz and worksheet on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: [20] 别忘了,你可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到本期节目的全部词汇,以及配套的测验和练习题。

text: But first, of course, Georgie, I have a question for you.
zh: [21] 不过,Georgie,我先有个问题要问你。

text: Charlotte Sterling -Reed is a child nutritionist and she has some tips for stress -free family meals.
zh: [22] 儿童营养师Charlotte Stirling-Reed为无压力的家庭聚餐提供了一些建议。

text: Which of these do you think she recommends? Is it a make sure your child eats five pieces of fruit and veg a day,
zh: [23] 你觉得她会推荐以下哪一项?a. 确保孩子每天吃五份水果蔬菜,

text: b don't pressure your child to eat everything on the plate, or c introduce your child to lots of new flavours quickly?
zh: [24] b. 不强迫孩子吃光盘子里的所有食物,还是c. 快速让孩子尝试许多新口味?

text: That's quite hard. They all seem like quite good tips.
zh: [25] 这有点难,它们听起来都是不错的建议。

text: I'm going to go with c introduce your child to lots of new flavours quickly.
zh: [26] 我选c,快速让孩子尝试许多新口味。

text: OK, we'll find out the answer later in the programme.
zh: [27] 好的,我们稍后在节目中揭晓答案。

text: Dr Anne Fishel is another expert in child nutrition.
zh: [28] Anne Fishel博士是另一位儿童营养专家。

text: She co -founded the Family Dinner Project, an organisation promoting children's healthy eating in the US.
zh: [29] 她共同创立了“家庭晚餐计划”,这是一个在美国推广儿童健康饮食的组织。

text: Here, Anne explains one reason why meal times can become stressful to Ruth Alexander for BBC World Service programme, The Food Chain.
zh: [30] 在这里,Anne向BBC世界服务节目《The Food Chain》的主持人Ruth Alexander解释了用餐时间为何会变得紧张的一个原因。

text: Many parents say dinner time can become a battleground.
zh: [31] 许多家长表示,晚餐时间可能会变成一个“战场”。

text: So why is it that what you're eating can trigger so much tension?
zh: [32] 那么,为什么吃什么会引发这么大的紧张情绪呢?

text: I think for small children, it's one of the few areas that they have control over.
zh: [33] 我认为对小孩子来说,这是少数几个他们能自己掌控的领域之一。

text: I mean, parents can cook what they want and serve it when they want, but it's up to kids to decide whether they're going to eat it.
zh: [34] 我是说,家长可以决定做什么菜、什么时候上菜,但吃不吃取决于孩子自己。

text: And for young kids, that's a big deal. They have some control.
zh: [35] 而对小孩子来说,这是件大事——他们由此获得了一些掌控感。

text: Dinner time often becomes a battleground, a place where argument or conflict happens.
zh: [36] 晚餐时间常常会变成“battleground”(战场),也就是发生争执或冲突的地方。

text: Anne thinks that's because it's one of the few areas where children have control.
zh: [37] Anne认为,这是因为这是孩子少数能掌控的领域之一。

text: For kids, controlling what they're willing to eat is a big deal – something of special importance to them.
zh: [38] 对孩子来说,能决定自己吃什么是件“大事”——对他们有特殊的重要意义。

text: If experts like Charlotte and Anne struggle at mealtimes, imagine how hard it could be if your child has a food allergy –
zh: [39] 如果像Charlotte和Anne这样的专家都在用餐问题上感到棘手,那么如果你的孩子有食物过敏,情况会有多难可想而知——

text: a condition where the body makes you sick after eating certain foods.
zh: [40] 食物过敏是一种病症,吃了某些食物后身体会不适。

text: That was the experience of Sharon Wee, a food writer from Singapore, who speaks here with BBC World Service programme, The Food Chain.
zh: [41] 这正是来自新加坡的美食作家Sharon Wee的亲身经历,她在BBC世界服务节目《The Food Chain》中讲述了这段经历。

text: My daughter was born with allergies and it was quite a struggle at first.
zh: [42] 我女儿生来就有过敏,一开始真的很难应付。

text: And to be honest with you, we found the winning formula, which was rice and beans.
zh: [43] 老实说,我们找到了那个“winning formula”(制胜配方),就是米饭配豆子。

text: She grew up eating rice and beans probably for the first five years of her life.
zh: [44] 她成长过程中,大概头五年都在吃米饭和豆子。

text: Sharon's daughter's allergies restricted what she could eat, making cooking for the whole family a struggle.
zh: [45] Sharon女儿的过敏限制了她能吃的食物,让给全家做饭变得很困难。

text: Luckily, Sharon discovered the winning formula, something her daughter could safely eat – rice and beans.
zh: [46] 幸运的是,Sharon找到了“winning formula”——她女儿可以安全食用的食物——米饭和豆子。

text: A winning formula is a method or strategy that produces successful results.
zh: [47] “winning formula”(制胜配方)指的是能带来成功结果的方法或策略。

text: So, Georgie, how can parents help their children develop good eating habits?
zh: [48] 那么,Georgie,家长该如何帮助孩子养成良好的饮食习惯呢?

text: Well, there are some things that our experts all agree upon.
zh: [49] 嗯,我们的专家们在一些方面达成了共识。

text: For one, give children a choice, for example by offering small healthy snacks and dips before the meal.
zh: [50] 首先,给孩子选择权,比如在正餐前提供一些健康的小零食和蘸酱。

text: And most importantly of all, create a stress -free atmosphere by not pressurising kids to finish everything on their plate.
zh: [51] 最重要的是,不要强迫孩子吃光盘子里的食物,营造一个没有压力的用餐氛围。

text: Right. Well, that also helps with your quiz question, Neil. So why not go ahead and reveal the correct answer?
zh: [52] 好的,这也解答了你的测验题,Neil。不如你直接揭晓正确答案吧?

text: I asked you what child food nutritionist Charlotte Stirling -Reed recommends for stress -free family meals.
zh: [53] 我之前问的是,儿童营养师Charlotte Stirling-Reed为无压力家庭聚餐推荐了哪一项建议。

text: Yes, I said c introduce your child to lots of new flavours quickly.
zh: [54] 是的,我当时选了c,快速让孩子尝试许多新口味。

text: I'm afraid you were wrong, Georgie. The answer is actually b don't pressure your child to eat everything on the
zh: [55] 恐怕你答错了,Georgie。正确答案其实是b,不强迫孩子吃光盘子里的食物。

text: plate. OK, let's recap the vocabulary we've learned, starting with ups and
zh: [56] 好的,我们来回顾一下今天学到的词汇,先从“ups and downs”开始——

text: downs – a phrase describing the mixture of good and bad things that happen to someone.
zh: [57] 这个短语用来形容一个人经历的好事和坏事交替发生的状态。

text: Your mood is how you feel at a particular time. A battleground is a place or situation where argument or conflict happens.
zh: [58] “mood”(心情)指你在某一时刻的感受。“battleground”(战场)指发生争执或冲突的地方或情境。

text: If something is a big deal, it's of special importance to you.
zh: [59] 如果某件事是“a big deal”,意思是它对你来说特别重要。

text: A food allergy is a medical condition where certain foods make your body sick.
zh: [60] “food allergy”(食物过敏)是一种医学病症,指某些食物会让你的身体不适。

text: And finally, a winning formula is a method or strategy that produces successful results.
zh: [61] 最后,“winning formula”(制胜配方)指能带来成功结果的方法或策略。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up, but if you'd like to learn more vocabulary
zh: [62] 我们的六分钟又到时间了,不过如果你想学习更多关于美食话题的词汇,

text: on the topic of food, you can head to our topic page on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: [63] 可以访问我们网站bbclearningenglish.com上的相关主题页面。

text: See you again soon, but for now, it's Goodbye.
zh: [64] 我们下次再见,现在先说再见啦。

text: 6 Minute English, from bbclearningenglish .com
zh: [65] 《六分钟英语》,来自bbclearningenglish.com。
